physical_translation: Surface properties are modulated by subsurface structure — lithology, mineralisation, structure, and fluids all alter surface observables
measurable_proxy: Reflectance anomaly, thermal anomaly, conductivity contrast, magnetic anomaly
notes: The most general and accurate framing. The entire GeaSpirit pipeline is built on this principle. No speculation needed; challenge is signal-to-noise.

physical_translation: Natural EM fields, thermal radiation, and gravity field are passively modulated by subsurface physical properties
measurable_proxy: Magnetic field anomaly, thermal IR radiance, Bouguer gravity deviation
notes: Correct but easily misread as intentional transmission. These are passive physical phenomena. The phrase is valid only if 'emits' means 'modulates ambient fields'.

physical_translation: Impedance contrast at subsurface boundaries modulates propagation of seismic and electromagnetic waves — measurable as standing wave ratios or apparent resistivity
measurable_proxy: H/V spectral ratio (passive seismic), MT apparent resistivity, EM spectral anomaly
notes: Technically accurate for H/V and MT methods. The term 'resonance' here is a geophysical term of art, not a metaphor. Access is the blocker, not the physics.

intuitive_phrase: An operator notices something in the area
physical_translation: Experienced operators unconsciously integrate geological cues: vegetation anomalies, colour changes, drainage patterns, surface texture, and moisture indicators
measurable_proxy: NDVI anomaly, spectral index anomaly, terrain derivatives, drainage density asymmetry
notes: The most directly GEE-testable translation. Operator pattern recognition encodes real geological signal — the task is to quantify what they are seeing into satellite features.

physical_translation: Current physical properties — thermal inertia, magnetic susceptibility, bulk density, porosity — encode the geological history of a site
measurable_proxy: Thermal inertia index, magnetic anomaly residual, density anomaly (gravity), porosity proxy (SAR moisture)
notes: Geology is history made solid — the speculation is whether the relevant signatures survive to the surface at detectable resolution. Thermal history proxy partially tested (Phase 5).

physical_translation: Anomalous surface properties — colour, texture, roughness, moisture content — are measurably different from surrounding background
measurable_proxy: Multispectral anomaly index, SAR backscatter texture, DEM roughness derivative, NDVI local deviation
notes: Direct translation into remote sensing anomaly detection. Already partially implemented in baseline spectral pipeline. Roughness and SAR texture are untested additions.

physical_translation: Geothermal gradient, radioactive decay heat, and exothermic chemical reactions in sulphide ore bodies produce measurable heat and radiation signals
measurable_proxy: Near-surface heat flow, airborne gamma-ray spectrometry, radon gas flux
notes: Physical mechanisms are real. Practical access is fully blocked — no open satellite heat flow data exists at deposit scale. Airborne radiometrics are government-held. Monitor for open data releases.

physical_translation: Altered rock and fault zones change local groundwater flow paths and surface soil moisture distribution
measurable_proxy: Soil moisture anomaly (SMAP), spring and seep locations, drainage pattern asymmetry, TWI (topographic wetness index)
notes: Strong physical basis. Hydrology fusion validated at multiple zones (Phase 9, 11, 12). SMAP is coarse (36km) but directionally useful. DEM-derived drainage is free and high-resolution.

physical_translation: Alteration minerals — iron oxides, clay minerals, gossans, silicification halos — produce diagnostic spectral signatures measurably different from unaltered host rock
measurable_proxy: Iron oxide ratio, clay index, hydroxyl absorption index, gossan spectral score
notes: The most directly validated intuition in this list. Core of the GeaSpirit spectral baseline. Already in production. EMIT adds hyperspectral precision for porphyry-specific mineral identification.

physical_translation: No credible physical proxy for the proprioceptive or intuitive sensation exists — the experience likely reflects unconscious integration of visual and geological cues by the observer
measurable_proxy: N/A — the operator's specific observations (what they saw, heard, smelled) may be encodable; the pulling sensation itself is not
notes: The experience is real and should be respected as operator expertise. The productive response is to interview the operator and extract the specific cues they observed — those observations can then be measured. The sensation itself cannot.
